title: FIFA Venue Inspection Tour Continues Across US Host Stadiums
detail: FIFA's final venue inspection tour — running April 14–28 across all 16 host cities — continued its review of US host stadiums, with inspectors assessing stadium readiness, pitch configuration progress, and matchday infrastructure. SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles, where Iran plays on June 15, and MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, site of the July 19 Final, are among the venues receiving the most intensive assessment as the 55-day countdown to kickoff continues. FIFA technical staff are also verifying broadcast infrastructure, dressing room standards, and field drainage.

media.caption: FIFA technical team inspects US host stadiums during the April 14–28 final venue assessment tour

title: USMNT World Cup Roster Debate Intensifies With 55 Days to Kickoff
detail: Sports analysts published a wave of USMNT World Cup roster projections as the May 26 announcement deadline came into focus. Following the 2–7 aggregate across friendlies against Belgium (2–5) and Portugal (0–2) in March, and with Patrick Agyemang ruled out with a ruptured Achilles, Pochettino faces a significant attacking depth problem. Key debates center on Christian Pulisic's lingering form concerns after 8 games without a club goal, the right goalkeeper (Steffen vs Turner), and whether emerging forwards like Daryl Dike or Josh Sargent can step up. The USMNT's next match is May 31 vs Senegal, five days after the roster is announced.

media.caption: USMNT World Cup roster analysis with 55 days to kickoff — Pulisic form, Agyemang injury concerns dominate debate

title: Infantino Defends World Cup Ticket Pricing at New York Economic Summit
detail: FIFA President Gianni Infantino publicly defended the 2026 World Cup's controversial ticket pricing at a New York economic summit on April 17, calling the United States 'a very special market' and describing the World Cup as FIFA's 'only source of income every four years.' Secondary market prices for the USMNT opening match vs. Paraguay reached $1,359 for the cheapest available ticket; Final tickets exceeded $25,000 on secondary platforms. FIFA introduced a limited $60 Supporter Entry Tier in response to public outrage, though this covers only 1.6% of total seats. Cat 1 Final tickets have a face value of $10,990. Infantino's defense came amid sustained pressure from fan groups, US Congress, European Parliament, and the FSE/Euroconsumers EU antitrust complaint filed April 3. The European Commission confirmed receipt and assessment of that complaint but has not opened a formal investigation.

media.caption: FIFA President Infantino addresses ticket pricing criticism at New York economic summit

title: Three Palestine FA Officials Denied Canadian Visas to Attend April 30 FIFA Congress in Vancouver
detail: Three senior Palestine Football Association officials — including PFA president Jibril Rajoub — were denied Canadian visas ahead of the April 30 FIFA Congress in Vancouver, according to CBC Sports, Morocco World News, and Pulse Ghana. The PFA had planned to attend the Congress to formally respond to FIFA's ruling on Israeli settlement clubs. The visa denials prevent Palestinian football leadership from participating in the 76th FIFA Congress, which will also address the Iran World Cup participation question and vote on hosts for the 2031 and 2035 Women's World Cups. The PFA has formally asked FIFA to intervene with Canadian immigration authorities. The incident adds political tension to what is already a contentious Congress agenda, coming 11 days before the Vancouver gathering.

media.caption: Palestine FA president Jibril Rajoub among three officials denied Canadian visas for the April 30 FIFA Congress in Vancouver
